title: XPS Fitting Studio reference-data schema
description: Schema for the version-controlled XPS reference dataset under data/xps/. Three document kinds: a sources registry (sources.json), photoelectron element files (elements-*.json), and the Auger line file (auger-lines.json). Hierarchy: element -> orbital family -> individual transition -> optional chemical-state references. Every numeric value's record carries a source id resolving in sources.json. Semantic rules the schema cannot express (unique ids, source-id resolution, region min<=max, spin-orbit reciprocity, file-role/transition-type agreement) are enforced by the loader (xps_reference.py).

curation_status.description: curated: all listed values verified against the cited sources. machine: values are NIST-evaluated reference energies recovered by dual machine extraction and corroborated, but NOT human-verified (see the per-transition tier + the elements-machine.provenance.json sidecar). partial: listed values verified, but known lines are missing (see curation_notes). uncurated: placeholder entry, no trustworthy open data curated yet — families may be empty; the UI must not present uncurated elements as authoritative.

family.description: Orbital family label: a subshell ('4f', '3d', '1s') for photoelectron files, or an Auger group ('LMM', 'KLL') for auger-lines.json.

orbital.description: Subshell with j where split ('4f7/2'), bare subshell for s lines ('1s').

nominal_be_ev.description: Reference binding energy in eV in the app's corrected-BE frame. Source-invariant after calibration: drawn at this value directly, never shifted by ccShift and never moved by the reference-source selector.

spin_orbit.description: Doublet linkage; explicit null for singlets (s lines) so curators must decide rather than omit.

orbital.description: Auger transition label, e.g. 'L3M4,5M4,5' or the family shorthand 'LMM'.

auger_ke_ev.description: Nominal KINETIC energy in eV — the source-invariant quantity for Auger lines. The apparent BE on a given source is computed at display time as photon_energy - auger_ke_ev - work_function (vgd_parser.py convention); it is never stored. nominal_be_ev is deliberately not an allowed property on Auger records.

expected_region_ev.description: Orientation range in KINETIC energy eV (same frame as auger_ke_ev); converted to apparent BE at display time.

transitionTier.description: Provenance tier of an individual transition. Absent => curated (the default for hand-curated element files). 'machine' = NIST-evaluated value recovered by corroborated machine extraction, not human-verified (elements-machine.json). 'legacy' is reserved for the legacy survey markers (which live in legacy/ and are tagged client-side, not via this field).

sourceId.description: Citation id; must resolve to an entry in sources.json (loader-enforced).

splitting_ev.description: Energy separation to the partner line in eV; positive on both members of the doublet (loader enforces existence + reciprocity).

area_ratio.description: Area of THIS line divided by area of the partner line (e.g. 4f7/2 vs 4f5/2 -> 4/3 on the 7/2 record, 3/4 on the 5/2 record).

basis.description: Provenance of the range. observed-reference-range: spanned by tabulated values across chemical states in the cited source(s). source-reported: the source itself states a range. conservative-fallback: no reliable range data; a deliberately wide orientation window. This is an ORIENTATION range for survey identification, NOT a claim that every chemical state falls inside it.

visibility.description: QUALITATIVE expected-visibility labels per X-ray source — curator judgement from reference survey spectra, NOT computed intensities (no Scofield/RSF/IMFP derivation in v1). 'uncurated' = not yet assessed for that source (UI falls back to the AlKa assessment, else treats as weak). 'unknown' = visibility genuinely not determinable from the available data (legacy markers); rendered at full visibility and NEVER auto-classified as weak — no major/moderate/weak value is invented. 'machine-unassessed' = same default-show treatment for machine-tier (NIST-evaluated, dual-corroborated) transitions whose intensity was never assessed; kept distinct from 'unknown' so the UI can label it 'intensity not assessed (machine-source-corroborated)'.

satellite_offset_ev.description: Offset of an associated satellite from this state's main line, in eV (positive = higher BE).
